name: Old-Fashioned Fish Chowder
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Turkey, fish or vegetable stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the vegetable oil in a large, heavy saucepan over low heat.
2. Add the onions and sauté for 10 minutes, or until golden but not brown.
3. Add the baking potatoes (cut into 1/4-inch cubes), dried thyme, and turkey stock. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
4. Add the cod or haddock fillet, cover, and simmer gently for another 10 minutes.
5. Add the milk, salt, and black pepper to taste. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the mixture is thoroughly hot.
6. Gently flake the fish with a fork into bite-sized pieces.
7. Ladle the chowder into soup bowls and sprinkle with paprika.
8. Serve immediately. Makes 6 servings.
Approximate nutritional analysis: 234 calories per half-cup serving, 20g protein, 22g carbohydrates, 7g fat (27% of calories), 1g fiber, 39mg cholesterol, 186mg sodium, 35% of the Daily Value for phosphorus.
